subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request from u_9981 (senior-engineer) to db-payments-primary against policy registry: verify on-call authorization, P1 incident justification, access scope bounds, and time-limited approval conditions

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier because this requires evaluating multiple policy dimensions (on-call role validation, incident severity classification, access scope governance, and time-bound approval conditions) against a policy registry. The decision involves multiple applicable rules (role-based access control, emergency protocol exceptions, and audit requirements), justifying mid-tier reasoning. No decomposition needed: this is a single coherent governance decision, not multiple independent checks. No cross_recon is required because this is a policy evaluation, not data reconciliation.

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) has an active P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042, satisfying POL-501's incident ticket requirement. POL-502's dual-approval requirement is waived under the declared P1 incident exception, but access must remain scoped to incident investigation only. POL-503 requires mandatory audit logging and post-incident review within 24 hours of access grant. All three conditions are satisfiable, permitting time-limited emergency read_write access with enforced constraints.
applied_constraints: Access is time-limited: must expire automatically within 4 hours of grant or upon P1 incident closure (whichever is sooner).
Access scope is restricted to tables and schemas directly relevant to incident INC-20241215-0042 investigation; unrestricted schema-wide access is not authorized.
All queries and write operations executed under this emergency grant must be fully logged to the audit trail in real-time.
Post-incident mandatory review of all access activity must be completed within 24 hours of incident closure per POL-503.
User u_9981 must confirm on-call status and incident ownership in the ticketing system (INC-20241215-0042) before access is provisioned.
A DBA or security officer must be notified of this emergency grant at time of issuance.
Any data modifications must be documented with justification within incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 immediately after execution.
Access will be automatically revoked and flagged for review if no activity is recorded within 30 minutes of provisioning.
